qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Remote code execution via script execution engine
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding exploits /api/runscript via Module._compile() code injection when authentication is DISABLED (default config). The advisory describes authentication bypass via the heartbeat refresh endpoint to mint admin JWTs when secureEnabled=true. These are different endpoints, different root causes (code injection vs authentication bypass), and opposite authentication conditions (disabled vs enabled). The advisory itself notes that CVE-2025-69970 covers the 'auth disabled by default' issue, which is distinct from this advisory's heartbeat JWT minting bypass.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes an authentication bypass via the heartbeat refresh endpoint that allows minting administrator JWTs when secureEnabled=true (CWE-285/CWE-287). The rce-via-script-engine finding exploits the /api/runscript script engine via Module._compile() when authentication is disabled — a different endpoint, different root cause, and opposite auth condition. No finding involves the heartbeat refresh endpoint or JWT minting logic that the advisory identifies as the root cause.
evidence: Advisory root cause: authentication bypass via heartbeat refresh endpoint to mint admin JWTs when secureEnabled=true, patched in commit fe82348d160904d0013b9a3e267d50158f5c7afb.
rce-via-script-engine root cause: code injection via /api/runscript using Module._compile() when auth is disabled (default config). Different endpoint, different mechanism, opposite auth condition.
The advisory itself distinguishes this vulnerability from CVE-2025-69970 (auth disabled by default), noting 'GitHub incorrectly stated this vulnerability is identical to CVE-2025-69970.'
